title: Defining Functions
objective: Define and call reusable functions with parameters and return values.
concepts: Calling functions with positional vs keyword args

exercise.description: Define compute_bmi(weight_kg, height_m) and test on 3 people. Write a greet(name, greeting='Hello') function.

homework: Write a function that takes a list of numbers and returns (min, max, average) as a tuple.

problems.q: Write a function celsius_to_fahrenheit(c) that converts and returns the value.
Write a function with a default argument that can be overridden.
problems.hint: def f(x, multiplier=2): return x * multiplier

example1.title: Basic function

example2.title: Default parameters
example2.code:
def greet(name, greeting='Hello'):
    return f'{greeting}, {name}'
print(greet('Alice'))      # Hello, Alice
print(greet('Bob', 'Hi'))  # Hi, Bob

application: Functions modularize code—data cleaning, feature engineering, and model evaluation are typically separate functions.
caseStudy: A ML pipeline has functions: load_data(), preprocess(), train_model(), evaluate(). Each is tested independently and composed in main().
